# Copilot Instructions

## Repository Overview

This repository contains a Terraform module that provisions the AWS infrastructure required for the [TKD Competition Registration](https://github.com/audioboxer217/gdtkd_registration) application. It is managed via Terraform Cloud under the `OKTKD` organization with workspaces tagged `tkd_reg` and `infra`.

## Key Technology Stack

- **Infrastructure as Code**: Terraform (HashiCorp) with AWS provider `~> 5.0`
- **State Management**: Terraform Cloud (`app.terraform.io`)
- **Cloud Provider**: AWS (us-east-1)
- **Pre-commit hooks**: `terraform_fmt` and `terraform_docs` via [pre-commit-terraform](https://github.com/antonbabenko/pre-commit-terraform)
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions (`.github/workflows/terraform.yml`) running `terraform fmt -check` and `terraform test`

## AWS Resources Managed

- **SQS**: `processing_queue` and `failed_registrations_queue` (DLQ)
- **DynamoDB**: `registrations_table`, `auth_table`, `lookup_table`
- **S3**: `config_bucket`, `badges_bucket`, `profile-pics_bucket`, `public_media_bucket` — all with versioning enabled
- **Route53**: Hosted zone and ACM DNS validation records
- **ACM**: TLS certificate with DNS validation
- **Cognito**: User pool, user pool client, user pool domain, and Admins user group

## Coding Guidelines

- All Terraform files must be formatted with `terraform fmt` before committing.
- Resource documentation is auto-generated by the `terraform_docs` pre-commit hook and inserted between the `<!-- BEGINNING OF PRE-COMMIT-TERRAFORM DOCS HOOK -->` and `<!-- END OF PRE-COMMIT-TERRAFORM DOCS HOOK -->` markers in `README.md`. Do not edit that section manually.
- Use `local.common_tags` for all resource `tags` attributes to ensure consistent tagging.
- Prefer `PAY_PER_REQUEST` billing for DynamoDB tables.
- S3 buckets should have versioning enabled.
- Config JSON objects stored in S3 must use `server_side_encryption = "AES256"`.
- Tests are written using the native `terraform test` framework (`.tftest.hcl` files) and run via `terraform test -compact-warnings`.

## Running Tests Locally

```bash
terraform init
terraform fmt -check
terraform test -compact-warnings
```

## Variable Conventions

- Required competition-specific variables (e.g., `competition_name`, `domain_name`, `reg_close_date`) have no defaults.
- Resource name variables follow the pattern `<resource>_name` (override) and `<resource>_prefix` (default prefix used when name is not set).
- Sensitive values (API keys, passwords) are declared as Terraform variables and stored as secrets in Terraform Cloud.
